raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "assertive declaration of personal agency and sole responsibility for one's life circumstances",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "an individual's condition is alterable",
    "only internal factors (thoughts, will, actions) can produce such alteration",
    "external forces lack independent causal power over one's condition",
    "the self possesses sufficient agency to enact change without external aid"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally consistent as a motivational axiom within mind-over-circumstance philosophy, but rests on an absolute exclusion of external causation that is not logically required",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "the audience seeks empowerment through radical self-responsibility and external constraints are minimal or surmountable by attitude and effort",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "external factors (systemic barriers, random events, others' actions) exert decisive causal influence that the statement categorically denies"
}
